title: Logical dependency DAG for RiemannHypothesisViaWarpedStationarity.tex (height-adapted version)
description: Machine-checkable encoding of the per-zero, height-adapted lemma chain in the standalone paper 'Zeros of the Riemann Zeta Function on the Critical Line via the Height-Adapted Unitary Warping of the Hardy Z-Function' culminating in Theorem thm:main (Riemann Hypothesis). For each nontrivial zero ρ=β+is of ζ with 0<β<1, the construction uses a per-zero height-adapted admissible shift c(s)=ϑ'(s)+K(s) where K(s):=sup_{t∈Ω_{δ_ρ}}|ϑ'(t)−ϑ'(s)|+1 and Ω_{δ_ρ}:={|Im t|≤1/2−δ_ρ} with δ_ρ:=(1/2)min(β,1−β). Each node declares its direct dependencies. The DAG is acyclic; topological sort yields a valid proof order. Every non-axiom node must be derivable from its predecessors using only the cited analytic inputs.

AX-Digamma.name: Digamma series and its imaginary part
AX-Digamma.statement: ψ'(w) = ∑_{k≥0}(w+k)^{-2} for w ∉ {0,-1,-2,...}; in particular Im ψ'(¼+it/2) < 0 for t > 0 so ϑ''(t) = −¼ Im ψ'(¼+it/2) > 0 for t > 0

AX-Olver.name: Olver Stirling expansion for ϑ and ϑ'
AX-Olver.statement: ϑ(t) = (t/2)log(t/(2π)) − t/2 + O(1) as t→+∞; ϑ'(t) = ½log(t/(2π)) + O(t^{-2}); holomorphic extension to the strip |Im t|<1/2 with the same leading asymptotics and O(|t|^{-1}) off-axis correction
AX-Olver.source: Olver, Asymptotics and Special Functions, Ch. 8 §4.3

AX-IFT.name: Inverse function theorem and smoothness of ϑ
AX-IFT.statement: ϑ ∈ C^∞(ℝ), holomorphic on |Im t|<1/2; a C^∞ bijection with nowhere-vanishing derivative has C^∞ inverse; a holomorphic map with nonvanishing derivative on a strip is a local biholomorphism
AX-IFT.source: Rudin, Principles of Mathematical Analysis, Thm. 9.24

AX-Zreal.name: Z is real on ℝ
AX-Zreal.statement: Z(t) = e^{iϑ(t)}ζ(½+it) is real-valued for t ∈ ℝ; Z(−t) = Z(t); Z is holomorphic on |Im t|<1/2

AX-FE.name: Functional equation of ζ on the critical line
AX-FE.statement: ζ(½−it) = e^{2iϑ(t)} ζ(½+it) for t ∈ ℝ; |e^{iϑ(t)}| = 1 so ζ(½+it₀) = 0 ⇔ Z(t₀) = 0 with matching multiplicities; for a nontrivial zero ρ=β+is, Z has a zero at t₀ = −i(ρ−½) = s+i(½−β) on its holomorphic extension
AX-FE.source: Riemann 1859; Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. 2

AX-HL.name: Hardy–Littlewood second moment
AX-HL.statement: ∫₀^T |ζ(½+it)|² dt = T log(T/2π) + (2γ_Euler − 1)T + O(T^{1/2} log T)
AX-HL.source: Hardy–Littlewood, Acta Math. 41 (1918), 119–196; Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. VII Thm. 7.3

AX-RS.name: Riemann–Siegel formula with phase-derivative remainder bound
AX-RS.source: Edwards, Riemann's Zeta Function, §7.4 Theorem 7.1; Gabcke Dissertation Göttingen 1979, Satz 7.1; Berry, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 450 (1995), eq. (18)

AX-Bochner.name: Wiener Theorem 25 (generalized Bochner for time-average WSS)
AX-Bochner.statement: If f:ℝ→ℂ is continuous with time-average covariance c_f(η) existing for every η and continuous at 0, then c_f is the Fourier transform of a finite non-negative Borel measure
AX-Bochner.source: Wiener, Acta Math. 55 (1930), Thm. 25; Rudin, Functional Analysis, 2nd ed., Thm. 1.4.3

AX-WK.name: Wiener Theorem 29 (spectral measure via windowed Fourier transform)
AX-WK.statement: For time-average WSS f with covariance c_f = Fourier transform of μ_f, and K_T(μ):=(2π)^{-1}∫_0^T f(u)e^{-iμu}du, one has for bounded Borel E⊂ℝ: μ_f(E) = lim_{T→∞} (2π/T) ∫_E |K_T(μ)|² dμ

AX-PW-WSS.name: Paley–Wiener for time-average WSS functions with compactly supported spectral measure
AX-PW-WSS.statement: If a locally square-integrable function f:ℝ→ℂ is time-average wide-sense stationary with covariance c_f(η) = ∫ e^{iηξ} dμ_f(ξ) and supp μ_f ⊆ [−σ,σ], then f admits an entire extension of exponential type ≤ σ satisfying |f(z)| ≤ μ_f(ℝ)^{1/2} e^{σ|Im z|} on ℂ
AX-PW-WSS.source: Yaglom, Correlation Theory of Stationary and Related Random Functions, Vol. I, Thm. 4.7.3; Bochner–Phillips, Ann. Math. 43 (1942), 409–418

AX-Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer–Levin Laguerre–Pólya criterion
AX-Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function F of exponential type ≤ τ whose autocorrelation measure (Fourier transform of |F|² as a tempered distribution) is non-negative, finite, and supported in [−2τ,2τ] belongs to the Laguerre–Pólya class; every complex zero of F is real
AX-Akhiezer.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, §V.4; Levin, Lectures on Entire Functions, Lecture 16 Thm. 3

AX-WSinc.name: Wiener–Plancherel sinc-reconstruction theorem
AX-WSinc.statement: For any time-average-WSS f with supp(μ_f)⊆[−Ω,Ω], lim_{T→∞} lim_{U→∞} U^{-1}∫_0^U |f(u_0) − (Ω/π)∫_{-T}^{T} sin[Ω(u_0−u)]/[Ω(u_0−u)] f(u) du|² du_0 = 0, with the two-sided interval replaceable by [0,V] at the cost of an O(V^{-1/2}) boundary error vanishing in the outer limit
AX-WSinc.source: Wiener, Acta Math. 55 (1930), Thm. 29 combined with Thm. 33; Doob, Stochastic Processes, Ch. XI §8 Thm. 8.1 (WSS form)
AX-WSinc.role: auxiliary: used only for the spectral-representation corollaries (cor:zeta-spec), not required for thm:main

AX-PlattTrudgian.name: Platt–Trudgian numerical verification of RH at low height
AX-PlattTrudgian.statement: Every nontrivial zero ρ = β+is of ζ with |s| < 3·10^{12} satisfies β = 1/2; in particular the finitely many zeros with |s| < 2π lie on the critical line unconditionally

AX-BanachAlaoglu.name: Banach–Alaoglu and Banach–Saks theorems
AX-BanachAlaoglu.statement: (BA) A norm-bounded family in L² has a weakly convergent subsequence. (BS) A weakly convergent sequence in L² has a subsequence whose Cesàro means converge in norm
AX-BanachAlaoglu.source: Rudin, Functional Analysis, 2nd ed., Thm. 3.15 and Thm. 3.13
AX-BanachAlaoglu.role: auxiliary: used only for the orthogonal Borel measure construction (thm:OrthPhi), not required for thm:main

def:shift.name: Height-adapted admissible shift
def:shift.statement: For the zero ρ=β+is, δ_ρ := (1/2) min(β,1−β), Ω_{δ_ρ} := {t∈ℂ:|Im t|≤1/2−δ_ρ}, K(s) := sup_{t∈Ω_{δ_ρ}}|ϑ'(t)−ϑ'(s)| + 1, c(s) := ϑ'(s)+K(s), Θ_s(t) := ϑ(t)+c(s)t

lem:Kfin.name: Finiteness of K(s)
lem:Kfin.statement: K(s) < ∞: ϑ' is holomorphic on Ω_{δ_ρ} (poles at t=±i/2 lie outside) and Olver's Stirling expansion gives ϑ'(t)−ϑ'(s) = (1/2)log(t/s) + O(|s|^{-1}+|t|^{-1}) bounded on the strip

lem:Repos.name: Re Θ_s' ≥ 1 on Ω_{δ_ρ}
lem:Repos.statement: For every t ∈ Ω_{δ_ρ}, Re Θ_s'(t) ≥ 2ϑ'(s) + 1 ≥ 1, provided |s| ≥ 2π; zeros with |s| < 2π are handled by Platt–Trudgian

cor:biholo.name: Θ_s is a biholomorphism of Ω_{δ_ρ}
cor:biholo.statement: Θ_s is a biholomorphism of Ω_{δ_ρ} onto its image; its restriction to ℝ is a C^∞ strictly increasing bijection ℝ→ℝ with Θ_s'(t)≥1 and C^∞ inverse Θ_s^{-1}

def:DOT.name: Omer–Torresani warping operator
def:DOT.statement: For a C¹ diffeomorphism γ:ℝ→ℝ with γ'>0, [𝒟_γ x](t) := √(γ'(t)) x(γ(t))

def:Y.name: Unwarped target Y
def:Y.statement: For the fixed zero ρ=β+is, Y(u) := [𝒟_{Θ_s^{-1}} Z](u) = Z(Θ_s^{-1}(u))/√(Θ_s'(Θ_s^{-1}(u)))

lem:Yreal.name: Y is real on ℝ
lem:Yreal.statement: Y(u) ∈ ℝ for every u ∈ ℝ

lem:HL.name: Hardy–Littlewood second moment (paper statement)
lem:HL.statement: ∫₀^T |ζ(½+it)|² dt = T log(T/2π) + (2γ_Euler − 1)T + O(T^{1/2} log T)

def:ta.name: Time-average structures
def:ta.statement: For f,g:ℝ→ℂ locally L², ⟨f,g⟩_ta := lim_{U→∞} U^{-1}∫_0^U f(u)conj(g(u))du, ‖f‖_ta² := ⟨f,f⟩_ta, c_f(η) := lim_{U→∞} U^{-1}∫_0^U f(u)conj(f(u+η))du
def:ta.role: auxiliary: pure notation for time-average inner product, norm, and covariance; depends only on the measure-theoretic facts asserted by the Hardy–Littlewood moment axiom that justify existence of the relevant time averages

thm:Ywss.name: Time-average wide-sense stationarity of Y
thm:Ywss.statement: c_Y(η) exists for every η ∈ ℝ and is independent of the origin of the time average; in particular c_Y(0) = 2

cor:Cramer.name: Wiener–Bochner spectral representation of c_Y
cor:Cramer.statement: There exists a finite non-negative Borel measure μ_Y on ℝ with c_Y(η) = ∫ e^{iηξ} dμ_Y(ξ), μ_Y(ℝ) = c_Y(0) = 2

def:KTS.name: Windowed Fourier transform and power spectral density
def:KTS.statement: K_T(μ) := (2π)^{-1} ∫_0^{Θ_s(T)} Y(u) e^{-iμu} du; S(μ) := limsup_{T→∞} 2π|K_T(μ)|²/Θ_s(T)

thm:bandlim.name: Band-limitedness of the windowed Fourier transform
thm:bandlim.statement: For every μ ∈ ℝ with |μ| > 1, S(μ) = 0

def:PhiT.name: Pre-limit density Φ_T
def:PhiT.role: auxiliary: supports the orthogonal Borel measure Φ construction; not required for thm:main

lem:Parseval.name: Parseval identity for Φ_T
lem:Parseval.role: auxiliary: supports thm:OrthPhi; not required for thm:main

thm:OrthPhi.name: Orthogonal Borel measure Φ on [−1,1]
thm:OrthPhi.statement: There exists a complex Borel measure Φ on [−1,1] with (i) Φ_T ⇀ Φ weakly in L²(ℝ), supp(Φ) ⊆ [−1,1]; (ii) |dΦ(ξ)|² = dμ_Y(ξ), ∫_{[-1,1]} |dΦ|² = 2; (iii) Y(u) = ∫_{-1}^1 e^{iξu} dΦ(ξ) for every u ∈ ℝ
thm:OrthPhi.role: auxiliary: explicit spectral synthesis for ζ on the critical line; not required for thm:main

thm:PW.name: Paley–Wiener entire extension of Y
thm:PW.statement: Y extends to an entire function of exponential type ≤ 1 with |Y(z)| ≤ μ_Y(ℝ)^{1/2} e^{|Im z|} = √2 e^{|Im z|}

thm:Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer–Levin Laguerre–Pólya criterion (paper statement)
thm:Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function of exponential type ≤ τ whose autocorrelation measure is non-negative, finite, supported in [−2τ,2τ] lies in the Laguerre–Pólya class; all complex zeros are real

thm:LP.name: Reality of zeros of Y
thm:LP.statement: Every zero of the entire extension of Y is real

cor:zeta-spec.name: Exact spectral formula for ζ on the critical line
cor:zeta-spec.statement: For every t ∈ ℝ, ζ(½+it) = e^{-iϑ(t)} √(Θ_s'(t)) ∫_{-1}^1 e^{iξΘ_s(t)} dΦ(ξ) = e^{-iϑ(t)} √(Θ_s'(t)) Y(Θ_s(t)); the sinc-kernel truncation F_T(t) := π^{-1} ∫_0^{Θ_s(T)} sin[Θ_s(t)-u]/[Θ_s(t)-u] Y(u) du satisfies lim_{T→∞} lim_{U→∞} U^{-1} ∫_0^U |F_T(t) - Y(Θ_s(t))|² dt = 0
cor:zeta-spec.role: auxiliary: analysis/synthesis identity for ζ; not required for thm:main

cor:invert.name: Inversion formula
cor:invert.role: auxiliary: analysis operator for the spectral synthesis; not required for thm:main

thm:zero-bij.name: Zero-set bijection Z ↔ Y on Ω_{δ_ρ} via holomorphic warping
thm:zero-bij.statement: t₀ ∈ Ω_{δ_ρ} is a zero of Z of order m if and only if Θ_s(t₀) is a zero of Y of order m; proof uses biholomorphism of Θ_s with nonvanishing Θ_s' on Ω_{δ_ρ}

cor:Zreal.name: Reality of zeros of Z in Ω_{δ_ρ}
cor:Zreal.statement: Every zero of Z in Ω_{δ_ρ} lies on ℝ

thm:main.name: Riemann Hypothesis
thm:main.statement: Every nontrivial zero ρ of ζ satisfies Re ρ = 1/2. Proof by contradiction: if ρ=β+is with β≠1/2, then t₀:=s+i(1/2−β) lies in Ω_{δ_ρ} (since |Im t₀|=|1/2−β|=1/2−min(β,1−β)<1/2−δ_ρ) and is a zero of Z by AX-FE, forcing t₀ ∈ ℝ by cor:Zreal, hence β=1/2

notes: Per-zero, height-adapted construction: for each nontrivial zero ρ=β+is, the strip Ω_{δ_ρ} and shift c(s)=ϑ'(s)+K(s) are tailored so Re Θ_s' ≥ 1 holds on Ω_{δ_ρ} by triangle inequality.

Primary proof chain: def:shift → lem:Kfin, lem:mono → lem:Repos → cor:biholo; def:DOT → lem:unitary; cor:biholo + lem:unitary → def:Y → lem:Yreal; def:Y + lem:HL + lem:RS → thm:Ywss → cor:Cramer; def:Y + lem:RS → thm:bandlim → cor:supp; thm:Ywss + cor:Cramer + cor:supp → thm:PW → (with lem:Yreal + thm:Akhiezer) → thm:LP; def:Y + cor:biholo → thm:zero-bij → cor:Zreal → (with AX-FE + t₀∈Ω_{δ_ρ}) → thm:main.

The spectral-representation branch (thm:OrthPhi, cor:zeta-spec, cor:invert) is a separate corollary stream not required for thm:main; it provides the explicit analysis/synthesis identity for ζ on the critical line.
Axioms: AX-Digamma, AX-Olver, AX-IFT, AX-Zreal, AX-FE, AX-HL, AX-RS, AX-Bochner, AX-WK, AX-PW-WSS, AX-Akhiezer, AX-WSinc, AX-PlattTrudgian, AX-BanachAlaoglu.

The contradiction in thm:main uses t₀:=s+i(1/2−β)=−i(ρ−1/2), which is a zero of Z by the functional equation and lies inside Ω_{δ_ρ} since |1/2−β|=1/2−min(β,1−β)<1/2−(1/2)min(β,1−β)=1/2−δ_ρ; cor:Zreal then forces Im t₀=0, i.e. β=1/2.
